mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate signals easier financial conditions and a lower equity discount rate, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect is confirmed when 20-day returns are strongest after sustained rate cuts and weaker when rates are flat or rising.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher after FEDFUNDS declines than after flat or rising readings, or if the signal's rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent level marks deteriorating household income and demand, which should pressure earnings expectations and generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect is strongest when unemployment breaks above a recent trend rather than merely fluctuating at low levels.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not produce negative median 20-day SPX returns, or if positive returns occur in more than 60% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS growth and PAYEMS growth strengthen together, the signal indicates broadening consumer spending supported by job creation, which should raise S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The mechanism fails if sales improve without payroll support or payrolls improve while retail sales contract.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint high-growth state of RSAFS and PAYEMS does not outperform either series alone in predicting positive 60-day SPX returns, or if interaction terms add no incremental predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: A downturn in INDPRO signals weakening industrial output and corporate revenue momentum, which should translate into lower S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect is most visible when production falls below its prior two-month average.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 2-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if below-trend INDPRO episodes fail to produce negative median 20-day SPX returns, or if the signal's hit rate is no better than 50% out of sample.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in HOUST below its recent baseline marks a housing-demand slowdown that often precedes weaker consumption and risk appetite, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The pattern is testable by comparing returns after housing starts contractions versus expansions.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day SPX returns are not lower after housing-starts contractions than after stable or rising readings, or if the sign flips in more than one rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A high FEDFUNDS rate combined with elevated CPIAUCSL implies restrictive real financing conditions and policy pressure on valuation multiples, which should weigh on S&P 500 returns over the next month. The interaction matters because high rates are most bearish when inflation remains elevated, not when inflation is already falling sharply.
failure_condition: Reject if the high-rate/high-inflation state does not produce lower 20-day SPX returns than other states, or if adding the interaction term does not improve out-of-sample performance.

mechanism_narrative: A low UNRATE environment combined with rising PAYEMS indicates a resilient labor market and supports household spending, which should generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next week. The signal is confirmed when payroll gains appear without a concurrent jump in unemployment.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE below 5.0% and PAYEMS rising month over month
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not show positive median 5-day SPX returns, or if the joint condition underperforms PAYEMS alone in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A GDP slowdown in GDPC1 combined with weak retail sales in RSAFS indicates broad demand deceleration that should depress equity prices over the next quarter. The mechanism fails when GDP softens but consumer spending remains firm, because earnings expectations then do not deteriorate enough to move stocks lower.
failure_condition: Reject if low-GDPC1/low-RSAFS states do not underperform all other states in 60-day SPX returns, or if the combined signal has no incremental explanatory power over each proxy separately.
